questions: Step 3 (parallelize RPCs): Does `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` depend on sequential DB state? If the RPC computes position as `max(frame) + 1`, parallel calls could produce duplicate positions. Should we keep serial and just add a comment, or is there a batch RPC available?

Step 6 (extract ClipContextMenu): The existing ClipAction has no dedicated test file. Should I add tests for the extracted component, or is the existing integration coverage (via timeline editor tests) sufficient?

Are there any other selection surfaces planned beyond gallery + timeline? If so, the registerPeerClear one-slot pattern would need to become a multi-subscriber pattern. Current plan assumes two panels is the stable state.

success_criteria.criterion: All 10 existing test files listed in Step 8 pass without modification (unless the test itself needs updating for the change)
No behavioral changes to selection, context menu, shot creation, or drag-drop from the user's perspective
No files added except potentially ClipContextMenu if extracted within ClipAction.tsx (same file)
Unused type aliases ShotGenerationPreview and ShotGenerationRow are removed
Inline arrow function props in GenerationsPaneGallery are replaced with direct references
ClipAction.tsx context menu JSX is extracted into a named component (ClipContextMenu or similar) in the same file
hasDataTransferType in dragDrop.ts reduced from 4 fallback paths to 2
useModifierKeys avoids unnecessary re-renders when modifier state hasn't changed
AgentChat mergeSelectedClips has a comment explaining the generationId preference
ClipAction.tsx stays under 350 lines total (clip + extracted menu)
createShotWithGenerationsPath uses Promise.all if RPCs are confirmed independent, or has a comment explaining why serial is required
No new abstractions, helpers, or utility files created
Manual smoke test: select multiple gallery items, right-click, create shot, generate video — all work as before

assumptions: The two-panel selection model (gallery + timeline) is the stable architecture — no third selection surface is planned. registerPeerClear stays as-is.
useExternalDrop's 791-line file size is acceptable because the hook itself is small and the extracted functions are pure helpers. Splitting would scatter related logic.
SelectionContextMenu, useLassoSelection, and the GallerySelectionContext core logic are already clean and don't need changes beyond what's listed.
The AgentChat mergeSelectedClips function is intentionally simple (14 lines, private) and doesn't warrant extraction to a shared utility.
Cross-browser drag-drop compatibility for DOMStringList is no longer needed (Chrome < 72 is EOL). The simplification in Step 5 is safe.
The shotCreationTypes unused aliases (lines 144-145) have no external consumers — grep confirmed they're not imported anywhere.
